mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production indicates rising capacity utilization and near-term earnings expansion, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward 20-day forward returns show Rank IC < 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window when INDPRO is increasing.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 6.5% signals a tightening labor market that generates wage income growth and consumption strength, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE is below 6.5% over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A widening spread between consumer price inflation and the nominal federal funds rate creates negative real rates that drive capital reallocation from bonds to equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint z-score spread between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS yields Rank IC < 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession demand recovery, month-over-month increases in real retail sales signal accelerating consumer spending that outpaces current supply, pushing S&P 500 returns higher over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 recovered to 15932.9 by Q4 2013 suggesting post-recession expansion
failure_condition: Month-over-month RSAFS increases in post-recession periods precede positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 0.25% signals the onset of monetary tightening that raises equity discount rates and reduces excess liquidity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm broad labor market tightening that raises aggregate income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal produces Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5% signals weakening residential investment and downstream demand, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances following a 5% month-over-month drop in HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion, sustained above-trend industrial production growth causes capacity constraints and input cost inflation that squeeze corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth periods are associated with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases over a 3-year window.
